message:
feat(dcg): Epic 10 graduated response system + suggest-allowlist --apply UX

Adds the Graduated Response System (Epic 10) which lets dcg escalate
its reaction to repeated occurrences of the same destructive command
within a session, instead of always emitting a single hardcoded denial.
Also adds a non-interactive `--apply` flag to `suggest-allowlist` so
patterns can be promoted to the allowlist programmatically.

Graduated response (Epic 10):
- src/config.rs introduces `ResponseConfig` and `GraduationMode` (Paranoid,
  Strict, Standard, Lenient, WarningOnly, Disabled), exposed under a new
  top-level `[response]` config section, plus per-severity overrides in
  `SeverityOverrides`.
- src/evaluator gains `GraduatedResponse` (Allow / Warning / SoftBlock /
  HardBlock), `BypassMethod` (None / Force / Env), and
  `determine_graduated_response(...)` which combines mode, severity, and
  per-session occurrence count to decide the response level.
  `EvaluationResult` carries `graduated_response` and `bypass_method`
  optional fields so downstream renderers can react.
- src/output/escalation.rs is a new module that formats stderr-friendly
  escalation messages for each level (Warning / SoftBlock / HardBlock),
  including occurrence counts, pattern id, severity label, reason,
  remediation steps, and bypass-applied notes. Re-exported from
  `src/output/mod.rs` and the crate root for use by CLI and TUI.
- src/cli.rs `Test` command gains `--force` to bypass a soft block from
  the graduated response system; `test_command(...)` threads the new
  `force: bool` argument through to evaluation. Robot/JSON output paths
  are unchanged.
- tests/graduated_response.rs is a new integration test exercising
  determine_graduated_response → apply_graduation → escalation messaging
  end-to-end across modes, severity overrides, soft-block bypass, and
  message formatting.
- tests/confidence_scoring.rs and src/output/test.rs initialize the new
  `graduated_response` and `bypass_method` fields on every constructed
  `EvaluationResult` literal so existing fixtures keep compiling.
- src/lib.rs re-exports `BypassMethod`, `GraduatedResponse`,
  `determine_graduated_response`, `EscalationContext`, and
  `format_escalation_message` from the crate root.

suggest-allowlist --apply (UX):
- src/cli.rs `SuggestAllowlistCommand` gains
  `--apply <comma-separated 1-based indices>` to skip the interactive
  approval prompt and write specific suggestions straight to the
  allowlist with a generated reason.
- New `apply_suggestions_by_index(...)` helper applies each index,
  records a `SuggestionAuditEntry { action: Accepted, ... }` for each
  successful add, prints `[N] Applied: <pattern> → <path>` per applied
  pattern and a final `<applied> applied, <skipped> skipped` summary.
  Out-of-range indices and "already in allowlist" cases are skipped
  rather than aborting the batch.
- tests/suggest_allowlist_e2e.rs adds `apply_flag_writes_to_allowlist`
  which seeds history fixtures, runs `--apply 1`, and verifies that the
  project-level `.dcg/allowlist.toml` is created with the expected
  pattern.

This commit is additive: existing config files, allowlist files, JSON
schemas, and tests continue to work without changes.

files.patch:

         skipped_due_to_budget: false,
         branch_context: None,
         session_occurrence: None,
+        graduated_response: None,
+        bypass_method: None,
     }
 }
